title: Bitwarden: error classifier uses brittle string-pattern matching
description:
## Problem
classify_bitwarden_sdk_error() in secretx-bitwarden/src/lib.rs identifies transient vs permanent errors by matching on error message substrings (e.g. checking for 'rate limit', 'timeout', 'network' in the Display string). The Bitwarden SDK does not expose typed error variants for these cases.

## Risk
If the SDK changes error message wording, rate-limit and network errors silently re-classify as Backend (permanent), disabling caller retry logic. This is a silent correctness regression, not a compile error.

## Options
1. Inspect SDK source for typed error variants and use those instead of string matching.
2. Add a comment listing the exact strings matched and the SDK version they were verified against.
3. Add an integration test that triggers a rate-limit scenario.

## Acceptance
Either typed matching implemented, OR comment with SDK-version-pinned string list and a TODO to upgrade when SDK provides typed variants.
